title: dalea carthagenensis subsp. domingensis
intro: Dalea carthagenensis subsp. domingensis, also known as Dominican prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the Caribbean and Southeastern United States. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dalea carthagenensis subsp. domingensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal usage.
